query: Generate a handoff payload for the next agent working on this feature.
why: Direct request for a handoff payload — the primary job of the agent-handoff skill.

query: I need to transfer this task to another agent — create the state payload.
why: Explicit request to transfer work with a structured state payload.

query: Before the next agent picks this up, write the structured handoff document.
why: Direct request for a structured handoff document for successor agent.

query: Hand off this in-progress auth implementation to the next agent.
why: Explicit instruction to hand off in-progress work to another agent.

query: Create the context payload so the successor can continue where I left off.
why: Request for a context payload whose purpose is successor continuation.

query: Write a README for this project.
why: README writing is documentation, not an agent-to-agent handoff.

query: Summarize this conversation for the user.
why: Conversation summaries for human readers are not agent handoff payloads.
ADRs are project documentation, not handoff payloads for successor agents.

query: Write a commit message for these changes.
why: Commit messages are git history entries, not inter-agent handoff state.

query: Generate API documentation for the endpoints I built.
why: API documentation is a deliverable, not an agent-to-agent state transfer.
